title: epipactis purpurata subsp. pseudopurpurata
intro: Epipactis purpurata subsp. pseudopurpurata, also known as the Purple Helleborine, is a rare and unique subspecies of orchid native to specific regions of the world. Traditionally, this plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Epipactis purpurata subsp. pseudopurpurata are attributed to its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant may be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its availability and use are often limited due to its rarity and potential toxicity.
